headline: Xi's reply to youth, Taiwan unification remarks, Nvidia-Intel clash dominate China's news
narrative: Chinese President Xi Jinping's letter to young guides at revolutionary museums, released ahead of Children's Day, underscores the regime's focus on ideological education from an early age. Meanwhile, a pro-independence Taiwan group, the "Happy Island Alliance", publicly stated its conditional acceptance of peaceful reunification, marking a notable shift in the island's political discourse. On the economic front, the SSE Composite fell 0.27% as tech stocks dragged, while a dramatic escalation in the chip war saw Nvidia and Intel directly invade each other's core markets. Socially, Alibaba's C2C platform Xianyu apologized after an AI misidentified a museum artifact photo as a commodity and listed it for sale, highlighting ongoing AI reliability concerns. The "Beng Laotou" (scam targeting the elderly) issue gained traction after a journalist went undercover in 500 chat groups, exposing systematic fraud. Parents' anxiety over kindergarten safety has led to aggressive real-time monitoring, adding pressure on teachers. The Children's Day theme also dominated festive content, with widespread calls to preserve innocence.
narrative_zh: 中共中央总书记习近平在儿童节前夕给革命纪念馆少先队讲解员回信，强调红色教育从娃娃抓起。岛内长期被视为“急独”的“喜乐岛联盟”公开表示有条件接受和平统一，引发震动。股市方面，上证指数微跌0.27%，科技股承压，英伟达与英特尔互相攻入对方传统芯片领域，竞争白热化。社会层面，闲鱼因AI误将文物照片识别为商品并上架而道歉，凸显AI可靠性问题；“崩老头”骗局经记者卧底500群曝光，引发广泛关注；家长频繁查看幼儿园监控导致教师压力增大。儿童节主题也占据热搜，呼吁保持童心。
